title: E6 portfolio sequencing study
eth_market_date: entry before 18:00 ET maps to the same calendar date; entry at or after 18:00 ET maps to the next calendar date

arms.S1_FIRST_PER_ETH_DATE: Chronological processing; execute the first E6 signal that is executable while flat for each ETH market date, then reject all later signals assigned to that ETH date
arms.S2_COOLDOWN_60: Current global sequencing plus a fixed 60-minute cooldown after every exit
arms.S3_SESSION_SLEEVES: Independent one-open-position state for Asia, London and New York; each executed trade contributes one-third of normal portfolio risk

s3_comparison_contract.raw_metrics: Report unscaled trade count, expectancy, win rate and profit factor
s3_comparison_contract.portfolio_metrics: Compare net return, drawdown, return/DD, yearly returns and paired ETH-date returns after multiplying every trade R by one-third
s3_comparison_contract.maximum_planned_risk: At most one-third risk in each of three sleeves, for one full risk unit when all sleeves overlap

required_outputs: trades and sequencing rejections by arm
year, half-year, session and direction attribution

classification.fresh_oos_challenger: At least 250 executed trades; material portfolio improvement; return/DD at least 1.15x S0; positive two-tick-each-side expectancy; acceptable yearly stability and concentration; familywise adjusted p <= 0.05; no unresolved implementation discrepancy
classification.shadow_only: Coherent but incomplete or underpowered effect
classification.reject: No meaningful portfolio benefit, materially worse efficiency, weak cost robustness, excessive concentration or implementation artifact

restrictions: No signal, E6 threshold, weekday, session, entry, stop, target or exit change
No alternative cooldown, daily trade count, sleeve weight or dynamic risk reallocation
No combination with P1-P3, R1-R2 or I1
No Pine, sizing or deployment promotion from historical evidence
